title: ByteWolf Detection Result v0.1
description: A versioned, validated detector output. It describes what a camera frame contained; it never commands. Consumers must distinguish valid, invalid, and missing results, and only act on a valid, fresh one.

captured_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset. When the frame was captured, never when it was processed: age is measured from this instant.

max_age_s.description: How long the result stays usable after captured_at. Past it the result is stale and must not be acted on.

validity.description: 'valid': the detector ran and trusts the result. 'invalid': the detector ran but does not trust it, e.g. a failure or an unreadable frame. 'missing': no frame was available, so there is no result. 'stale' is not declared here; it is derived from age.

source.description: What produced the result, e.g. 'gz mono_cam + stub'. Free text for audit, never for dispatch.

detections.description: The objects found. A valid result may be empty (nothing detected); an invalid or missing result carries none.

bbox.description: Pixel box in the frame, origin top-left.

then.description: Only a valid result may carry detections; an invalid or missing one must not, so absence cannot be read as 'nothing there'.
